name: Bee Agent Framework (IBM)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); pay for LLM API calls; IBM Granite models available free on HF

auth.notes: TypeScript framework; configure LLM provider API key in environment

performance.notes: Framework overhead negligible; all latency from LLM provider and tool execution

agent_readiness.notes: IBM's open-source TypeScript agent framework. ReAct, Granite-optimized agent patterns. MCP support. Code execution sandbox via E2B. Built-in memory management. LangChain-compatible tool integration. Designed for IBM Granite models but works with any LLM. Streaming support. Agent serialization for persistence.

gotchas: Younger project with smaller community than AutoGen or LangChain
Documentation still maturing — some features poorly documented
IBM Granite optimization means less tested with other LLMs
TypeScript-first: Python developers not the target audience
best_when: TypeScript agent development wanting IBM Granite model integration
IBM/watsonx ecosystem deployments needing open-source agent framework
Teams wanting TypeScript alternative to AutoGen or LangChain.js
MCP-native TypeScript agents with code execution capability
avoid_when: Python-first development stacks (use AutoGen, LangChain, CrewAI)
Teams needing large community support and ecosystem
Production deployments requiring long-term stability guarantees
summary: Bee Agent Framework is IBM's open-source TypeScript agent framework optimized for IBM Granite models. Supports ReAct patterns, MCP tool integration, E2B code execution sandbox, and agent serialization. Works with any LLM despite Granite optimization. Streaming support. Younger project with smaller community than LangChain.js or AutoGen. Best for TypeScript teams in the IBM/watsonx ecosystem.
